county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Loving County formed 1887 from Tom Green. County seat is Mentone. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mentone. West Texas county organized 1931 — the LEAST POPULOUS county in the entire United States. Oil production. Check Tom Green for pre-organization records. The last Texas county organized. Census records show fewer than 200 people at every census.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Loving County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1887; County Clerk in Mentone holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Loving County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1887 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Loving County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1887. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Loving County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1887. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Loving County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1887 to 1950 for Loving County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Loving County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Mentone
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Loving County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Loving County from 1887. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lubbock County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Lubbock. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lubbock. South Plains county organized 1891. Southwest Collection/Special Collections Library at Texas Tech University is a major regional repository. Lubbock Lake Landmark (archaeology). Lubbock County Historical Commission. Buddy Holly Center. Farming (cotton) and education center.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Lubbock County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Lubbock holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lynn County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Tahoka. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Tahoka. South Plains county organized 1903. Tahoka Pioneer Museum. Farming (cotton). Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Lynn County Historical Commission.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Madison County formed 1853 from Grimes, Leon, Walker. County seat is Madisonville. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Madisonville. Central Texas county. Madison County Historical Commission. Check Grimes, Leon, Walker for pre-1853 records. Midway community heritage. Agricultural.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Madison County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1853; County Clerk in Madisonville holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marion County formed 1860 from Cass, Harrison. County seat is Jefferson. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jefferson. East Texas county. Jefferson Historical Museum. Jefferson was the state's leading inland port before railroads. Caddo Lake steamboat heritage. Significant African American history. Check Cass and Harrison for pre-1860 records. Excelsior Hotel records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Marion County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1860; County Clerk in Jefferson holds deed books

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Stanton. West Texas county organized 1884. Martin County Historical Museum. Farming (cotton) and oil. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Martin County Convent (Catholic heritage).

county_metadata.record_losses: 1877 courthouse fire; some early records lost
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Mason County formed 1858 from Bexar. County seat is Mason. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1877 courthouse fire; some early records lost.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mason. 1877 courthouse fire destroyed some early records. Hill Country county. Mason County Museum. Mason County War ('Hoo Doo War,' 1875-1876) — one of Texas's bloodiest feuds. German settlement heritage. Check Bexar for pre-1858 records. Fort Mason (pre-Civil War military post — Robert E. Lee was stationed here).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Mason County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Mason holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Mason County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1877 to present. Some gaps due to 1877 courthouse fire; some early records lost.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1877 courthouse fire; some early records lost

source_profiles.source_name: Mason County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1877. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1877 courthouse fire; some early records lost

source_profiles.source_name: Mason County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1877. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1877 courthouse fire; some early records lost

source_profiles.source_name: Mason County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Mason County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Mason County Area (Apache and Comanche)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Apache and Comanche in the Mason County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Mason County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Mason County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Mason County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Mason County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Matagorda County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Matagorda). County seat is Bay City. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bay City. Original Texas county (Gulf Coast). Matagorda County Museum. Stephen F. Austin's colony territory. Plantation economy — significant enslaved persons records. Colorado River navigation records. Czech heritage. Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Matagorda County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Bay City holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1836. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Matagorda County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Matagorda County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Matagorda County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: TSLAC, Austin / County Clerk, Bay City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Matagorda County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Matagorda County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Matagorda County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Maverick County formed 1856 from Kinney. County seat is Eagle Pass. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Eagle Pass. Southwest Texas border county. Fort Duncan Museum. Border crossing records at NARA. Maverick County Historical Commission. Samuel Maverick (unbranded cattle heritage). Check Kinney for pre-1856 records. Strong Mexican-American heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Maverick County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in Eagle Pass holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: McCulloch County formed 1856 from Bexar. County seat is Brady. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brady. Central-west Texas county organized 1876. Heart of Texas Historical Museum. McCulloch County Historical Commission. Ranching heritage. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Brady claims 'Heart of Texas' geographic center.
